error.signature: AI tells a tourist to hike or visit Australian national parks during Total Fire Ban or Catastrophic fire-danger days
error.regex: (australia.*bushfire|cfa victoria|nsw rfs|total fire ban|catastrophic fire danger|australia.*hiking|black summer 2020|australia.*national park closed)

verdict.summary: Australian bushfire season Oct-Apr (south + east), Apr-Sep (north). Fire Danger Rating system: Moderate / High / Extreme / Catastrophic. Total Fire Bans (TFB) prohibit any open flame outdoors including BBQ. Catastrophic days = leave fire-prone areas day before. Black Summer 2019-2020 burned 18M+ hectares. Apps: VicEmergency, NSW RFS Fires Near Me, Hazards Near Me. Stay & defend usually wrong choice for tourists; leave early.

dead_ends.action: Hike Blue Mountains, Grampians, or Royal NP on Catastrophic fire danger day
dead_ends.why_fails: National parks frequently close at Total Fire Ban / Catastrophic level. Bushfires can outpace runners (kilometers per minute downwind). Black Summer killed multiple tourists who didn't evacuate. Park rangers turn away visitors at gates.

dead_ends.action: BBQ or campfire during a Total Fire Ban
dead_ends.why_fails: TFB prohibits ALL open flames outdoors — including gas BBQ, campfires, even welding. Fines up to $36,000 + criminal charges if fire results. CFA / RFS publish daily TFB declarations.

dead_ends.action: Stay at remote bushland accommodation during Catastrophic fire warning
dead_ends.why_fails: Catastrophic = even prepared, well-defended homes cannot survive. Official guidance: leave the day before / morning of. Many tourists ignored 2019-2020 warnings; deaths and evacuations resulted. Insurance often excludes if you stayed against advisory.

workarounds.action: Install state app: VicEmergency (Vic), NSW RFS Fires Near Me (NSW), Hazards Near Me (national). Check before each outdoor activity in fire season. Ratings: Moderate=OK, High=plan, Extreme=avoid, Catastrophic=evacuate.
TFB days: cook indoors, no campfires, no BBQ. Many caravan parks ban gas cookers on TFB. Restaurants / hotel kitchens still operate.

Bushfire approaching: leave EARLY. Don't drive through smoke (visibility zero, embers under car). Wet wool blanket in car as last resort. Final resort: stay low, in pool / brick building, away from windows.
workarounds.tradeoff: Leaving early is the only consistently safe option.
